pattern: Every adjacent swap moves exactly one ball past exactly one other, so the answer collapses to counting how many white balls sit to the right of each black ball.
hints: Grouping all the 1s to the right is the same problem as counting how "out of place" each ball already is - think about inversions.
For every 0 you encounter, how many 1s have already appeared before it? Each of those 1s needs exactly one swap to get past this 0.
Scan left to right keeping a running count of 1s seen so far; every time you see a 0, add that running count to the answer.

edge_inputs.description: already fully grouped, no swaps needed
completely reversed, maximum work for this length
all balls the same color across a longer run
one stray black ball far to the left of an otherwise sorted run

stress.description: max-length random binary string with roughly balanced 0/1 counts
stress.note: roughly 2^n possible strings; random sampling stresses the running-count approach at full length
